duels.objection:
You skim the surface, wave after wave,
The details you miss become problems you've paved.
Your spreadsheet's a lie, your dashboard's a show,
Without diving deep, you're the last one to know!

replies.text:
I checked all the numbers — they looked pretty clean,
The clearest of dashboards I have ever seen.
replies.comeback: Clearly clean. Not actually analyzed. Distinctions matter.

replies.text:
I built a six-pager with root-cause in place,
And traced every metric back to its source with grace.

My manager approved it, so clearly it's fine,
Deep dives are for people who don't have my shine.
replies.comeback: Approved by your manager, who also never read it. An impressive chain.

replies.text:
I dove to the data — I looked at the mean,
The median's okay and the mode's looking keen.
replies.comeback: Mean, median, mode. Without context, use case, or action. Brilliant.

duels.objection:
It's not my problem — that's what you say,
You pass the blame like it's Groundhog Day.
Ownership means you step up and lead,
Not wait for permission for every small deed!

replies.text:
I own it completely — my part and my piece,
The rest of the project can go as it please.
replies.comeback: You own your part. The rest of the project: entirely on its own.

replies.text:
I spotted the outage at midnight last night,
And stayed on the bridge call until we got it right.

I sent a Chime ping to flag up the dread,
And added myself to the calendar thread.
replies.comeback: A calendar invite to a crisis. Leadership by RSVP.

replies.text:
I plan to escalate to my skip-level next week,
With a three-slide summary for the update they seek.
replies.comeback: Escalating next week. The outage appreciates your schedule.

duels.objection:
Your vision is modest, it fits on a Post-It,
While rivals are running, your dreams are a closet.
Think bigger, think bolder, break open the box!
Or shuffle back home to your inbox of rocks!

replies.text:
My idea will scale to at least twenty users,
A niche little product for discerning choosers!
replies.comeback: Twenty users. Truly the addressable market of our time.

replies.text:
My roadmap targets a ten-billion TAM,
Built on a flywheel that outpaces the RAM.

I thought big last Tuesday from nine until ten,
And then I filed it and closed it again.
replies.comeback: An hour of big thinking, neatly filed. Ambition shelved under done.

replies.text:
My dreams are enormous and fill up the hall,
But data and planning aren't needed at all.
replies.comeback: Dreams without data. How refreshingly uncommitted.

duels.objection:
You wait for permission on everything small,
While windows close shut and opportunities call.
A two-way door swings — just walk right on through!
But you're still at the doormat, awaiting review!

replies.text:
I'll schedule a meeting to plan our next steps,
And pre-read the pre-read to manage my reps.
replies.comeback: A meeting to plan the meeting. The circle of corporate life.

replies.text:
Reversible actions still need sign-off from eight,
I can't move a muscle until approvals are great.
replies.comeback: Eight approvals. For a reversible two-way door. In an emergency. Noted.

replies.text:
I launched an experiment by the end of last week,
The data came back — the results are unique.

I biased toward action — I biased toward lunch!
Which technically counts as a meaningful crunch.
replies.comeback: Lunch is not a deliverable. However appetizing.

duels.objection:
You spent the Q3 budget on swag and on snacks,
While ROI crumbles from your lavish attacks.
We do more with less — it's a core pillar here,
But more of your spending means less of our year!

replies.text:
But morale needed lifting with that custom tote!
Without branded merchandise, teams can't stay afloat.
replies.comeback: Tote bags. The cornerstone of fiscal discipline.

replies.text:
I cut tooling costs from seven vendors to two,
And consolidated headcount to see the work through.

Frugality means never approving a spend,
I've zeroed the budget right down to the end.
replies.comeback: Refusing all budgets is anarchy with spreadsheets.

replies.text:
The snacks boost morale, which boosts productivity gains,
The ROI's clearly positive — just follow the chains.
replies.comeback: I will not be approving the pretzel-to-productivity conversion model.

duels.objection:
Your promises crumble like old meeting cake,
Your timelines are fiction, your updates are fake.
Trust must be earned with each action and word,
But your credibility? Frankly absurd!

replies.text:
I tell people exactly what they want to hear,
My trust scores go up every end of the year.
replies.comeback: Trust scores. Measured by people you've never disagreed with.

replies.text:
Trust is like Wi-Fi — sometimes it just drops,
I'll reconnect later once the interference stops.
replies.comeback: Your trust metaphor is as unreliable as your delivery dates.

replies.text:
I flagged the delay two full sprints before due,
With a mitigation plan and a fresh status view.

I earned trust by never admitting mistakes,
Projecting pure confidence — whatever it takes.
replies.comeback: Confidence without competence. A bold strategy. Let me know how it lands.

duels.objection:
Your bar is set low, it scrapes on the ground,
You ship it as good enough — look what you've found!
We raise the bar higher and higher again,
Not hand off half-baked work down the delivery chain!

replies.text:
I raised my bar once — it was exhausting, I'll say,
So I quietly lowered it before end of the day.
replies.comeback: A one-time bar raise followed by a permanent bar drop. Inspiring arc.

replies.text:
Standards are relative — mine just are mine,
And relative to nothing, my work is just fine.
replies.comeback: Standards relative to nothing. The philosophical foundation of mediocrity.

replies.text:
I rejected three drafts and revised the design,
Until every data point finally aligned.

My bar is so high that I check every word,
No document ships until spacing's preferred.
replies.comeback: Impeccably spaced documents full of wrong conclusions. A truly high bar.

duels.objection:
Your judgment is foggy, your instincts are off,
Great leaders have wisdom — you fumble and scoff.
Being right, and a lot, takes a calibrated mind,
Not hunches spun loose from assumptions you find!

replies.text:
I'm right 100 percent of the time,
My colleagues are simply not keeping in line.
replies.comeback: One hundred percent correct. Not even statistically plausible.

replies.text:
I stress-tested my thesis with three teams last week,
And updated my stance when the data did speak.

I look for opinions that mirror my own,
That way I stay right in a comfortable zone.
replies.comeback: An echo chamber of agreement. Efficiently and catastrophically wrong.

replies.text:
Being right is a spectrum — I'm on the good side,
Which is slightly more right than the ones who have tried.
replies.comeback: Slightly more right than people who are wrong. The bar has never been lower.

duels.objection:
Your process is tangled in seventeen steps,
You complicate simple with PowerPoint reps.
Simplicity is genius — complexity is shame!
If Einstein ran this project, you'd still be to blame!

replies.text:
I simplified boldly — I removed all the docs!
Now nothing is tracked and the process just rocks.
replies.comeback: Documentation: gone. Accountability: also gone. Bold simplification.

replies.text:
Complex processes need complexity — that's law!
You can't reduce nuance without finding a flaw.
replies.comeback: Complexity as doctrine. How many steps does it take to send an email?

replies.text:
I invented a simpler complicated way!
Six steps instead of eight — progress, I'd say.
replies.comeback: Six steps instead of eight. Progress requiring a microscope to observe.

replies.text:
I cut the approval chain from five steps to one,
And automated the handoff — the toil is done.

duels.objection:
Your output is noise, your launches are smoke,
Your OKRs limp and your roadmap's a joke.
Results are what matter — we measure what's real,
Not activity theater and performative zeal!

replies.text:
I was very busy — I had meetings all year!
My calendar's full — is that not results, dear?
replies.comeback: A full calendar. The ultimate lagging indicator of zero output.

replies.text:
My results are coming — they're nearly pre-ready,
I just need more time to keep progress steady.
replies.comeback: Nearly pre-ready. I'm adding that to our list of banned phrases.

replies.text:
Results are subjective — it all depends how you look,
I've documented everything neatly in a book.
replies.comeback: Reframing zero output as a documented success. Efficiently reframed.

replies.text:
I launched three features and closed out the year,
Each tied to a revenue goal — results are clear.
